label: GRC Analyst
description: Manages governance, risk, and compliance programs including IT audit, risk assessments, policy development, ISO 27001 ISMS implementation, business continuity, and regulatory compliance.

paths.name: ISACA Track
paths.rationale: The most recognized GRC path globally for audit and risk roles. CISA is the gold standard for IT audit; CRISC for IT risk management. Together they open the broadest range of GRC roles.
pros: CISA is the most recognized IT audit credential globally
CRISC is the most recognized IT risk management credential
Opens audit, risk management, and GRC management roles across all sectors
cons: Both require significant verified work experience

prerequisites.experience: CISA requires 5 years IT audit/control/security experience. CRISC requires 3 years IT risk management experience. Not entry-level credentials.

cert_sequence.notes: OCEG's GRC Professional certification — an accessible entry point for GRC practitioners building foundational framework knowledge. Covers GRC capability model, policy management, and compliance program structure. Good foundational credential while accumulating experience toward CISA.

The gold standard IT audit credential. Study with the ISACA CISA Review Manual and Hemang Doshi's practice questions. The exam covers IS audit process, IT governance, information systems acquisition, IS operations, and protection of information assets. Experience verification takes time — submit the application well in advance. CISA alongside Security+ satisfies most entry and mid-level GRC and audit analyst job requirements.

IT risk management capstone. Covers risk identification, assessment, response, and monitoring from an enterprise governance perspective. With CISA providing audit foundations, CRISC adds the risk quantification and management framework that GRC managers need. CISA + CRISC together is the most recognized combination for senior GRC and risk manager roles.

paths.name: ISO Framework Track
paths.rationale: For GRC analysts whose work centers on ISO 27001 ISMS implementation and management. Most relevant in European and Asia-Pacific markets, regulated industries, and organizations pursuing ISO 27001 certification.
pros: ISO 27001 LI and LA are widely recognized for ISMS implementation roles
PECB credentials are recognized across European and Asia-Pacific markets
Directly applicable to organizations undergoing ISO 27001 certification
Lower experience requirements than ISACA credentials
cons: Less recognized than CISA or CRISC in US market
More narrowly focused than ISACA track

prerequisites.experience: Some security or IT management experience is beneficial. Lower barriers than ISACA credentials.

cert_sequence.notes: The PECB ISO 27001 Foundation — introduces the standard structure, key requirements, and ISMS concepts. Quick entry point and often included in PECB training packages.

ISO 27001 Lead Implementer — covers ISMS design, implementation, and management in detail. The primary credential for practitioners leading ISO 27001 certification projects. Requires a 5-day training course from an accredited provider.

ISO 27001 Lead Auditor — covers the audit methodology for ISO 27001 ISMS assessments. Together LI + LA gives you both the implementation and audit perspective, making you capable of leading certification projects and conducting compliance audits. The most recognized combination for ISO 27001 consultants.

ISO 22301 Business Continuity Lead Implementer — complements the ISMS credentials with business continuity program design skills. ISO 27001 and ISO 22301 are frequently implemented together in enterprise environments, making this a natural extension credential.

paths.name: Government / DoD Track
paths.rationale: For GRC analysts targeting federal government, defense contractor, or FISMA-regulated environments. Prioritizes DoD 8140 approved credentials.
pros: CISA and CRISC are DoD 8140 approved
Directly applicable to FISMA, FedRAMP, and RMF compliance work
CISSP adds DoD 8140 coverage for senior GRC roles
cons: Slow path given experience verification timelines

prerequisites.experience: Security+ is commonly required as a baseline for government positions. Prior federal IT or security work is strongly advantageous.

cert_sequence.notes: The DoD-approved IT audit credential and the most recognized GRC credential in government environments. Start here after Security+.
DoD-approved risk management credential. CISA + CRISC satisfies most senior government GRC and risk analyst job requirements.
Adds the universal senior security credential that opens CISO-track and senior GRC management roles in government. CISA + CRISC + CISSP is the most complete DoD-approved GRC credential stack.
